## Topic: Project Identity and Purpose
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md

The JobDash Design System v1.0 is the UI kit and visual language for JobDash, a
job-application tracking product. It defines tokens, components, patterns,
illustrations, icons, and motion for the full web product, plus mobile views,
email templates, and status pages.

Scope: 53 sections across 10 groups:
  Foundation, Layout, Inputs, Surfaces, Data Display, Feedback, Navigation,
  Patterns, Interaction, Sample Screens

Targets WCAG 2.2 AAA for body text; AA-or-better for all UI elements.

---

## Topic: File Nature of Design Handoff
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md

The design_handoff/ files are interactive HTML prototypes created using inline
Babel JSX — NOT production code to be copied directly. They are high-fidelity
references: pixel-precise for colors, typography, spacing, shadows, motion
timings, focus rings, hover/active states, and content/copy.

The task is to recreate the system in the target codebase (React + TypeScript)
using established patterns. Tokens should be lifted into CSS custom properties.
The HTML's ad-hoc class names map cleanly to component props.

---

## Topic: Visual Vocabulary and Aesthetic Constraints
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md

The system has a deliberate aesthetic identity — deviations corrupt the brand:

1. Cream + ink + amber palette. Warm-neutral cream (not white), near-black ink
   (not pure black), amber as the only saturated brand accent. No additional
   accent colors introduced.

2. Borders over shadows. Cards use 1px var(--rule) border + cream-2 raised
   background. Drop shadows only for genuinely-floating elements (modals,
   popovers, command palette).

3. Display + mono pairing. Archivo display + JetBrains Mono labels (uppercase
   eyebrows, codes, numbers). Inter for body text.

4. Generous letter-spacing on display. Tightened -0.02em to -0.035em.

5. Always-on-cream surfaces. Page bg is cream — never pure white. This warmth
   is the most distinctive visual cue of the system.

6. AAA contrast measured and documented in ds-accessibility.jsx.

---

## Topic: Implementation Order Recommendation
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md

Recommended build sequence:
  1. Tokens first — port :root and body.dark CSS custom properties end-to-end.
  2. Foundation components — Button, Input, Select, Checkbox, Toggle, Badge,
     Chip, Card, Modal, Toast.
  3. Layout primitives — AppShell, AppBar, Sidebar, Footer.
  4. Feature patterns — Wizard, EmptyState, Coachmark, HoverCard, Lightbox,
     BottomSheet.
  5. Sample Screens last — integration test; if they look correct in both
     modes, system is assembled correctly.

For each component, reference the matching ds-*.jsx file for layout structure,
exact spacing/padding, and all states.

Test at each step: light + dark mode, keyboard navigation, prefers-reduced-motion,
axe-core scan.

---

## Topic: Font Assets
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md

Archivo (display), Inter (body), JetBrains Mono (mono) — all free Google Fonts.
Embed via @font-face or use the existing font loader in the codebase. No
proprietary or licensed fonts in the system.

---

## Topic: Icon Asset Strategy
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md

119+ icons as inline SVGs in ds-iconset.jsx. Conventions: 1.5px stroke, 24x24
viewBox, stroke="currentColor". Can be reproduced as React components (copying
SVG paths verbatim) or substituted with Lucide / Heroicons / Phosphor provided
line-weight matches to maintain visual unity. No raster icons.

---

## Topic: Illustration Asset Strategy
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md

24 spot illustrations in ds-illustrations.jsx as inline SVGs. Palette restricted
to cream + ink + amber system colors only. Can be exported as static SVG files
or implemented as React components. Used in empty states, onboarding, errors.

---

## Topic: Email Templates
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md

Three email templates: Welcome, weekly digest, deadline reminder. Table-based
HTML for email-client compatibility. Always light-mode regardless of app theme.
Email templates are out of scope for the React component library but are part
of the 53-section complete system.

---

## Topic: Mobile Views
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md

iOS frame examples provided in ds-mobile.jsx: list view, application detail,
add note flow. Mobile views are a design reference; the React component library
targets web (responsive). Bottom sheets are the primary mobile-specific pattern
that crosses into web.

---

## Topic: Status Pages
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md

404 and 500 pages. The 500 page is intentionally always-dark (locked #1c1917
background) regardless of app theme — it is a DarkSurface context.

---

## Topic: Theming Implementation Pattern
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/README.md

Key implementation rules:
- class="dark" on <body> activates dark mode via CSS cascade — no JS recompute.
- Surfaces that FLIP between modes: use var(--cream-2) bg + var(--ink) text.
- Surfaces that STAY DARK in both modes: hardcode #1c1917 bg + #f5f3f0 text
  (NOT token references). Prevents cream-on-cream failure in dark mode.
- Inverted chrome (dark-pill on light, light-pill on dark): pair var(--ink) bg
  + var(--cream) text — both flip together.
- Encode explicitly as: Surface (flipping), DarkSurface (always-dark),
  InvertedPill (paired chrome).
- User preference persisted in localStorage.

---

## Topic: Phase 24-27 Components Now Specified
source: classification files in .planning/intel/classifications/
  + 5 newly-classified SPEC docs in design_handoff/design-system/

The previously-flagged "missing specs" (see prior context: Reference Files Not
Classified in This Ingest) have been resolved. The following components now have
SPEC-level intel and are ready to plan:

From ds-navigation.jsx (SPEC) — Phase 24:
  TreeItem (recursive nested nav row with depth indentation, chevron, badge, count)
  CollapsibleSidebar (220/56px collapsing sidebar with logo + items + collapse toggle)
  SidebarNavSection / flat-sidebar variant (dot-indicator nav rows)

From ds-notifications.jsx (SPEC) — Phase 25:
  NotificationCenter (panel with grouped notifications, unread state, mark-all-read,
    per-item dismiss)
  NIcon (type-mapped icon helper: success/warning/error/info)
  InlineBanner (in-flow notification variant)

From ds-patterns.jsx (SPEC) — Phase 26:
  FileUploadZone (drag-drop drop zone with click fallback, animated per-file
    progress, file-list with thumbnail + extension badge + progress + remove)

From ds-mediacards.jsx (SPEC) — Phase 27:
  MediaCard (glass card with cover, optional badge, hover overlay action, body)
  GalleryCard (2x2 mosaic with overflow count tile)
  PlaceholderImg (design-time striped placeholder primitive — see CONSTRAINT-022)

From ds-status.jsx (SPEC) — Phase 27:
  StatusFrame (shared wrapper for status page demos)
  NotFoundPage (404 layout — display 56px headline, big "404" mark, sticker overlay,
    receipt card)
  ServerErrorPage (500 always-dark layout — see CONSTRAINT-020 / DECISION-036;
    hardcoded #1c1917/#f5f3f0)
  MaintenancePage (centered countdown layout with marquee tape banner)
  OfflinePage (cache-state layout with signal-rings illustration and pending-changes queue)

## Topic: Additional Components Found in ds-patterns.jsx (Out of Scope)
source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-patterns.jsx

ds-patterns.jsx contains FOUR additional pattern components beyond the FileUploadZone
that this ingest scoped for Phase 26:

  TimelineSection / Activity Timeline — vertical event timeline using .ds-timeline,
    .ds-tl-ev (with .upcoming, .done modifiers), .ds-tl-dot, .ds-tl-when, .ds-tl-body,
    .ds-tl-title, .ds-tl-desc; chips inside timeline items
  StepperSection / Stepper Wizard — multi-step form wizard with circular step
    indicators (28x28 var(--amber)/var(--ink)/var(--cream-2) bg by state),
    progress connector bars, Back/Next navigation, "Step N of M" label
  NotificationSection / Notification Inbox — alternative notification panel pattern
    (440px max-width) with type-specific tinted icon backgrounds and per-row
    click-to-mark-read interaction (overlaps in purpose with NotificationCenter
    from ds-notifications.jsx but uses different visual contract)
  FilterBarSection / Filter Bar — horizontal filter pill group with search input,
    pill toggle groups for stage and priority, Remote toggle, and Clear-all action

These were NOT in the manifest scope for Phase 26 (which was FileUploadZone-only).
They are flagged in the conflict report as INFO entries. To build them, add to a
future ingest manifest with appropriate phase mapping.

Notably, NotificationSection in ds-patterns.jsx and NotificationCenter in
ds-notifications.jsx are TWO DIFFERENT visual contracts for similar purpose. The
team should decide which (or both) to ship before either component plan starts.
This is logged as INFO, not WARNING, because Phase 25 only commits to the
ds-notifications.jsx contract.
